mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures aggregate consumer demand momentum. Rising retail sales signal robust nominal spending that supports corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observed months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals a deteriorating labor market. This deterioration reduces household income growth and consumer confidence, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered episodes over any 3-year rolling window, refuting the negative directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) alongside a stable or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) compresses real interest rates and raises inflation risk premia in equities. The joint condition, not either series alone, drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: In a 5-year rolling window, the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS adds less than 0.01 to explanatory R-squared versus the sum of individual series effects, or the joint signal Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial Production (INDPRO) growth forecasts positive S&P 500 returns only when residential construction activity (HOUST) exceeds 1,200 thousand units, confirming that supply-side output is underpinned by healthy demand rather than inventory accumulation. Below this threshold, the mechanism is inactive.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1200, positive changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of the triggered months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.0 percent signals a restrictive monetary environment that tightens financial conditions and elevates short-term discount rates. This restrictive stance produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Over a 2-year rolling window, S&P 500 5-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS > 2.0% are negative in fewer than 45% of instances, rejecting the negative directional prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates expanding labor income and aggregate demand. This always-on labor market momentum signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the macro regime.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reveals broad-based demand strength spanning durable goods and consumer spending. The interaction effect, which is stronger than either series in isolation, predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from HOUST and RSAFS underperforms the stronger of the two single-series signals in terms of Rank IC over a 4-year rolling window, indicating the interaction adds no value.

mechanism_narrative: Three-month momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns when the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 4.0 percent. A tight labor market confirms that production gains reflect genuine demand pressure rather than unsustainable inventory building.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE is below 4.0%, INDPRO momentum fails to achieve a Rank IC greater than 0.04 for predicting 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.
